name: EPITHELIOID HEMANGIOENDOTHELIOMA
definition: rare primary low-grade malignant vascular tumor of liver (soft tissue, skin, bone, lung)

demographics.Path: multifocal nodules varying in size from a few mm to several cm involving both lobes of
demographics.Histo: dendritic spindle-shaped cells + epithelioid round cells in a matrix of myxoid + fibrous

discriminating_features: coalescence of nodules into confluent masses
peripheral subcapsular growth (in 75%) without capsular bulge

general: coalescence of nodules into confluent masses
peripheral subcapsular growth (in 75%) without capsular bulge
US: individual / confluent nodules or diffusely heterogeneous echotexture
nodules with echogenicity unrelated to size:
hypoechoic ← central core of myxoid stroma
CECT: target / halo pattern = nonenhanced outer rim of avascular tissue + enhanced inner
peripheral + progressive centripetal enhancement with incomplete fill-in on delayed phase
enhanced / nonenhanced tumor center on delayed imaging
MR: "target" sign / concentric zones = alternating high- and low-signal-intensity rings on T2WI:
central zone of fibrosis / hemorrhage / necrosis
hyperintense center + thin rim of hypointensity on T2WI
multilayered targetlike appearance with prominent rimlike enhancement
"lollipop" sign = synchronous targetlike enhancement of tumor mass + along hepatic /
ANGIO: hyper- and hypovascularity (dependent upon degree of sclerosis + hyalinization)
invasion ± occlusion of portal + hepatic veins
NUC: decreased perfusion to central myxoid tumor portion + increased perfusion to cellular areas
photopenic defect on static sulfur colloid scan
moderate to intense uptake on PET

clinical.Prognosis: 20% die within 2 years, 20% survive for 5-28 years ± treatment
clinical.Rx: liver transplantation (without spread); radical resection; transarterial chemoembolization
